invariant: BF5 — new code must conform to the captured CONVENTION_BASELINE, not canon defaults
seed: overlay defects/convention-drift/tagService.py → src/freelancer_app/tags/tagService.py (new canon-default file: new top-level package, camelCase, swallows CT2 failures)
(authoritative blocking output of running CRITIQUE clean-room) = defects/convention-drift/critique.flagged.json → .build/slices/S5/critique.json
load_bearing_assertion: CRITIQUE verdict == 'blocked'. The drift manifests as two of CRITIQUE's five anti-cheat categories: gold-plating (an unowned new package `tags/` violating CONVENTION_BASELINE layout 'one package per component', tracing to no R*/AC*) + swallowed-failure (catches CT2 store-unavailable/constraint-violation → returns None). Golden critique verdict == 'clean', 0 issues.
separates_from_golden_by: critique verdict (clean→blocked) + issue_count (0→2) — the BF5 invariant.
note: CRITIQUE owns NO named 'convention-drift' category; BF5 drift surfaces through its gold-plating + swallowed-failure categories (an off-convention file is also an unowned/untraceable file). IMPLEMENT is the primary BF5 owner (conforms_to_conventions self-check + CONVENTION_BASELINE grounding); CRITIQUE is the hostile backstop that blocks the shipped drift.
e2e_validated: 2026-06-10 — clean-room step-runner ran prompts/04-build/CRITIQUE.md against the drift bench → verdict 'blocked', issues [gold-plating, swallowed-failure] on tags/tagService.py, both routed to IMPLEMENT. Golden bench → 'clean', 0 issues. Oracle separates.
